plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, as they are below stops.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single-name mandate.
Reduce QQQ and SMH exposure due to their downtrends and trim flags.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Initiate a new position in ZS at 8% for top-quality tech exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk significantly.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower drawdowns but also limited upside potential.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETFs and improve risk profile.
Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQ, QQQI, and SMH due to their downtrends and trim flags.
Initiate new positions in ZS and ASML, strong scanner setups, for quality growth.
Maintain a balanced approach, blending broad index with quality tech.
This plan focuses on risk-first growth while reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month growth, prioritizing risk management. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding high-quality growth setups. Expect moderate drawdowns and a balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stops and reallocate capital.
Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQ and SMH exposure due to their downtrends and trim flags.
Initiate new, larger positions in ZS, GUSH, and NVDA, leveraging high-scoring setups.
This plan leans into strong scanner setups and allows for increased concentration.
Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage magnified risks from this approach.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups. It embraces higher risk for potentially higher returns, but also significantly increases drawdown potential. Daily decay and rebalancing in leveraged ETFs (if added) amplify risks, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta names.
Trim QQQ and SPY to significantly reduce broad index exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on rotating into income-generating ETFs.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and to capture future opportunities.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth potential.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged holdings. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
